# Personalize Knock workflow emails with per-recipient trigger data (Python)

## Background

Your team uses https://knock.app for transactional notifications. Knock lets a single workflow trigger call notify multiple recipients, and each recipient can receive its own merged data via the inline-recipient `$trigger_data` field. You need to ship a Python script that defines (upserts) a Knock email workflow whose template renders per-recipient data, and then triggers it once for multiple inline recipients so each person receives an email customized with their role and dashboard URL.

The Knock account already has an email channel with key `mailtrap` configured for delivery. Inbound mail is captured through Gmail using sub-addressed inboxes. All actions in this task must be scoped to the current `run-id` so concurrent runs do not collide.

## Requirements

* Read `run-id` from the `ZEALT_RUN_ID` environment variable and use it to namespace every externally visible resource (workflow key, recipient identifiers, and recipient email addresses).
* Use the Knock Management API (raw HTTP from Python is fine) to upsert a workflow in the `development` environment, and activate it so the trigger endpoint can run it.
  * Workflow key: `per-recipient-alert-${run-id}`.
  * The workflow must contain exactly one channel step that targets the email channel with key `mailtrap`.
  * The email template MUST render the per-recipient values from the trigger data. Specifically:
    * The subject line MUST include the merged `data.alert_type` and the recipient's `data.role`.
    * The body MUST include the recipient's `data.role` and `data.dashboard_url`.
* From a Python script, trigger the workflow exactly once using the Knock backend SDK (`knockapi`) and provide:
  * A shared `data` payload containing `alert_type` (use the literal string `security_breach`).
  * Two inline recipients identified entirely on the trigger call (no separate identify step required):
    1. id `owner-${run-id}`, name `John Hammond`, email `${GMAIL_USER_NAME}+r1-${run-id}@gmail.com`, with `$trigger_data` containing `role = "Park Owner"` and `dashboard_url = "https://jurassicpark.com/dashboard/owner-${run-id}"`.
    2. id `paleo-${run-id}`, name `Ellie Sattler`, email `${GMAIL_USER_NAME}+r2-${run-id}@gmail.com`, with `$trigger_data` containing `role = "Paleobotanist"` and `dashboard_url = "https://jurassicpark.com/dashboard/paleo-${run-id}"`.
* Log the resulting workflow key and `workflow_run_id` to a log file so the verifier can confirm the trigger happened.

## Implementation Hints

* The Management API requires the account-level service token `KNOCK_SERVICE_TOKEN` (header `Authorization: Bearer ...`) and only supports writes in the `development` environment. After upserting the workflow, you must activate it so it can be triggered. The CLI is not required — you can do everything from Python with `requests` or `urllib`.
* The backend trigger SDK is `knockapi` (v1+). Authenticate with `KNOCK_API_TOKEN` and use `client.workflows.trigger(...)`. Inline recipients are passed as full dicts so Knock can identify them just-in-time.
* Knock email templates use Liquid; reference per-recipient values with `{{ data.role }}` and `{{ data.dashboard_url }}`. The `$trigger_data` on each inline recipient is merged with the top-level `data` for that recipient's run.
* Gmail sub-addressing means each `+suffix` address still lands in the same `${GMAIL_USER_NAME}@gmail.com` inbox, so you can fan out to multiple `+r1-${run-id}` / `+r2-${run-id}` addresses without provisioning new mailboxes.

## Acceptance Criteria

* Project path: /home/user/myproject
* Ensure the script is executed and the artifacts exist. Do not mock Knock or Gmail.
* Log file: /home/user/myproject/output.log
* The workflow MUST be created in Knock with the exact key `per-recipient-alert-${run-id}` and a single email channel step bound to the `mailtrap` channel.
* The workflow MUST be active in the `development` environment so it can be triggered.
* The Python script MUST call `client.workflows.trigger` (or the equivalent HTTP `POST /v1/workflows/{key}/trigger`) exactly once with two inline recipients carrying the per-recipient `$trigger_data` described above.
* The log file MUST contain a line in the format `Workflow key: <key>` and another line in the format `Workflow run ID: <workflow_run_id>` printed after a successful trigger.
* After the trigger runs, the Gmail inbox MUST receive one email per recipient delivered through the `mailtrap` channel. Each email's subject and body MUST render that recipient's own `role` and `dashboard_url` (e.g. the email sent to `+r1-${run-id}` mentions `Park Owner` and the owner dashboard URL; the email sent to `+r2-${run-id}` mentions `Paleobotanist` and the paleo dashboard URL).

# Management API - Documentation | Knock Docs
created: 2023-01-15, last updated: 2026-04-16, [Read more](https://docs.knock.app/developer-tools/management-api)

The Knock management API provides you with a programmatic way to interact with the resources you create and manage in your Knock dashboard, including workflows, templates, and translations.
It's separate from the Knock API and only provides access to a limited subset of resources.
You can use the Knock management API to:
- Create, update, and manage your Knock workflows and the notification templates within those workflows.
- Create, update and manage your email layouts.
- Create and manage the translations used by your notification templates.
- Create, update, and manage your partials.
- Commit and promote changes between your Knock environments.
...
Currently, we do not ship SDKs that wrap the management API but we do ship a CLI for easily interacting with the resources in your Knock account.
...
To preview a template, you use the preview template endpoint exposed by the management API.
This API endpoint accepts a workflow key, and a step reference (`ref`) for the step that contains the template you want to preview.
Using the endpoint, you pass through details about the `recipient`, `actor`, `tenant`, and any other data variables that are used within the template.
The endpoint will return a JSON object with fully rendered template generated using the data you provided.

# Management API | Knock Docs
created: 2021-01-01, last updated: 2025-07-10, [Read more](https://docs.knock.app/mapi-reference/templates)

The Knock management API provides you with a programmatic way to interact with the resources you create and manage in your Knock dashboard, including workflows, templates, layouts, translations and commits.
It's separate from the Knock API and only provides access to a limited subset of resources.
You can use the Knock management API to:
- Create, update, and manage your Knock workflows and the notification templates within those workflows.
- Create, update and manage your email layouts.
- Create and manage the translations used by your notification templates.
...
- Commit and promote changes between your Knock environments.
...
## Upsert a workflow#
Updates a workflow of a given key, or creates a new one if it does not yet exist.
Note: this endpoint only operates on workflows in the
`development` environment.
### Endpoint
### Path parametersworkflow_keystring
The key of the workflow.
### Query parametersenvironmentstring
The environment slug.annotateboolean
Whether to annotate the resource.
Only used in the Knock CLI.commitboolean
Whether to commit the resource at the same time as modifying it.commit_messagestring
The message to commit the resource with, only used if
`commit` is
`true`.

# Implementing Knock
created: 2023-01-15, last updated: 2026-03-31, [Read more](https://docs.knock.app/tutorials/implementation-guide)

Knock’s APIs and developer tools make it easy to migrate your notification templates, delivery logic, recipient data, and user preferences into Knock.
...
- A Management API that allows you to work programmatically with the same resources that you create in your Knock dashboard (like Workflows and their associated message templates, email Layouts, and Translations).
- A command line interface (Knock CLI) that wraps the Management API, allowing you to work with your dashboard resources from the command line while you’re developing.
- Bulk endpoints to upsert large amounts of data in a single API request (more on specific endpoints below).
...
Next, you’ll start building your Workflows.
Workflows in Knock serve as containers for all of the logic and message templates associated with a given notification in your system.
When you’re ready to start sending notifications, you’ll do so by triggering these workflows.

# Identifying recipients - Documentation | Knock Docs
created: 2023-01-15, last updated: 2026-05-17, [Read more](https://docs.knock.app/managing-recipients/identifying-recipients)

There are three ways to identify recipients:
- **Direct identification**.
Make a single server-side API request (
```
PUT /v1/users/{user_id}
```
) to upsert one recipient.
- **Inline identification**.
Upsert recipients by including recipient data in other server-side API requests (e.g.,
```
POST /v1/workflows/{workflow_key}/trigger
```
) or when initializing the
…
client-side.
- **Bulk identification**.
Make a single server-side API request (
```
POST /v1/users/bulk/identify
```
) to upsert many recipients at once.
...
Make a server-side API request to upsert a single recipient in Knock.
...
Client-side identification is handled automatically when you initialize the
...
with user data provided.
...
The bulk identification endpoint enables you to upsert many recipients in a single server-side request.
...
When getting started with Knock, you'll likely have existing recipients to migrate into Knock:
- **Quick start**.
Use inline identification to start calling your workflows without any prerequisite calls to the Knock API.
- **Data import**.
Use our bulk identification to import all recipient data into Knock first.

The Knock management API provides you with a programmatic way to interact with the resources you create and manage in your Knock dashboard, including workflows, templates, layouts, translations and commits.

# Migrate from Braze to Knock
created: 2023-01-15, last updated: 2026-05-19, [Read more](https://docs.knock.app/tutorials/migrate-from-braze)

- A Management API that enables you to work programmatically with the resources that you can also create directly in your Knock dashboard (like workflows and their associated message templates, email layouts, and translations.)
...
- Bulk endpoints that enable you to upsert large amounts of data in a single API request (more on specific endpoints below.)
